# Project State — Yelli

# Updated: 2026-05-23 12:10 GMT+8 by CLAUDE_CODE

PHASE: **(fix/auth-bypass-app-env-guard) HOTFIX READY on `fix/auth-bypass-app-env-guard`** — pre-merge. Discovered during first-attempted Playwright smoke (option (b) of post-merge follow-up plan) that `/api/auth/providers` returned only `{credentials}` despite `AUTH_BYPASS_FOR_E2E=true` + `NODE_ENV=development` correctly set in the rebuilt container. Forensic grep of `/app/apps/web/.next/server/chunks/*.js` found `NODE_ENV:"production"` baked in as 3 hardcoded string literals (chunks 3289 / 7947 / 984) — webpack's DefinePlugin inlined `process.env.NODE_ENV` at `next build` time as `"production"`, so the dual-gate `AUTH_BYPASS_FOR_E2E === true && env.NODE_ENV !== "production"` constant-folded to `false` regardless of container runtime env. Verified that custom env vars (DATABASE_URL, AUTH_SECRET, REDIS_URL) all survive as runtime `process.env.X` reads — only NODE_ENV is in DefinePlugin's static-replacement target. Fix: switched prod-guard from NODE_ENV to APP_ENV — a project-controlled var added to env.ts serverSchema with same `z.enum(["development", "staging", "production"])` validation. APP_ENV is set per environment by Phase 3 env-file templates (.env.dev → "development", .env.staging → "staging", .env.prod → "production") and survives bundling as runtime read. Defense-in-depth preserved: dual-gate with AUTH_BYPASS_FOR_E2E still requires both flags to be misconfigured simultaneously to weaken auth. 3 files modified (env.ts +9, auth-bypass.ts ~6 predicate signature + docstring rewrite, auth-bypass.test.ts replace_all NODE_ENV→APP_ENV across 9 test cases). 287/287 ✓ unchanged. pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors / typecheck ✓. 2 lessons.md writes — NEW 🔴 [[webpack-define-plugin-trap]] at head documenting the inlining behavior with empirical bundle-grep proof + the operational rule "never gate runtime security logic on env.NODE_ENV in Next.js bundled code"; UPDATED 🔴 [[auth-bypass-prod-guard]] reflecting APP_ENV semantics with a "WHY NOT NODE_ENV" cross-reference to the new trap entry. Inline Opus 4.7 controller (Tier 1; ~70K context — includes original Playwright smoke setup + bundle-grep forensics + hotfix implementation).
